data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, independently repo-corroborated this pass.

REPO VERIFICATION (this pass):
- Dual path confirmed: typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` is registered at typed_dispatch.py L56 ((outbound,update)) and reconcile.py L75, while the PRODUCTION outbound dispatch routes through the batch path `batch_dispatch.py update_one` (its own comment L387-388: "the production outbound dispatch routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf _apply_outbound_update"). This is exactly the drift 33d0 collapses.
- Representation asymmetry confirmed: in the typed leaf, `labels_applied` is `list[str]` (apply_outbound.py L245 `labels_applied: list[str] = []`, L257/L260 append `f"+{name}"`/`f"-{name}"`, L389 `"labels_applied": labels_applied`) while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325/L278), and inbound `links_applied` is also an int (apply_inbound.py L469) — the int "parity" model 2359 cites.
- The existing no-op skip-guard at apply_outbound.py L372-378 uses TRUTHINESS (`not labels_applied`), NOT `== 0`.
- The PRODUCTION batch path carries NO per-sub-op counter at all: update_one (batch_dispatch.py L501-559) applies comments/links/labels but never tallies them, and applier.py L816-821 RECON batch_outcome emits only action/key/error. So when 33d0 collapses onto the surviving (production/batch) path, its parity gate MUST MINT the per-sub-op result representation for the survivor.

ONE high-confidence finding remains (a representation conflict at the 33d0→2359 seam that the declared `2359 depends_on 33d0` edge sequences but does NOT resolve). This is the symmetric counterpart of the finding already filed on 2359; recorded here for this pairing's record from 33d0's side.

FINDING — conflicting assumptions: 33d0 owns the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload (it retargets the typed-leaf tests at the survivor and its parity gate asserts the survivor applies every sub-op the retired path did), in which `labels_applied` is list-valued while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints; downstream sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) layers a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` count-canary over those very keys assuming a uniform integer count, and 33d0 fixes no canonical label representation for the single survivor.
- finding: 33d0 What ("Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path") + its parity gate ("assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — fields, comments, labels, links, parent") make 33d0 the owner of the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload. Because the production batch path has no counters today, 33d0 must establish that payload's shape for the survivor. The typed-leaf candidate shape it would carry over is NOT uniformly count-shaped (`labels_applied` list[str]; links/comments ints). Sibling 2359 then surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as links/comments and defines a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary citing int parity. 33d0 nowhere states the canonical label representation, so the survivor 2359 instruments is handed an ambiguous label signal: keep the list (2359's `applied == 0` predicate is ill-typed over a list — `[]` ≠ `0`, a non-empty list is truthy) or flip to an int (breaks any exact-list label assertion 33d0 just retargeted). Either way a shared contract is left unowned at the seam between THIS child (owns the survivor's payload) and 2359 (consumes it for the canary). The depends_on edge sequences the work (33d0 lands first) but does not by itself decide the label representation.
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (behavioral parity — surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did, before deletion), 33d0 AC3 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — asserting each sub-op took effect); cross-checked against 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied) + 2359 AC2/AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 count canary); parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path" (33d0) vs parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly" (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope (retarget typed-leaf tests; parity gate over the surviving payload); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (append `+`/`-` label strings), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L278/L325 (`comments_applied`/`links_applied` int), L372-378 no-op guard (truthiness `not labels_applied`, not `== 0`); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py L469 (inbound `links_applied: int` — the parity model 2359 cites); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py update_one L501-559 (production path applies comments/links/labels but tracks NO per-sub-op counter); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py L816-821 (RECON batch_outcome emits only action/key/error); typed_dispatch.py L56 + reconcile.py L75 (typed-leaf route) vs batch_dispatch.py L387-388 (production routes through the legacy batch path)
  - evidence: repo confirms the surviving-applier candidate payload carries `labels_applied` as list[str] and links/comments_applied as ints; the production batch path carries NO per-sub-op counter, so 33d0's parity gate must mint the label representation for the survivor; 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then layers a count-based `computed>0 && applied==0` invariant on that very key citing an int parity model; the no-op guard 33d0 may carry over uses truthiness, not a `== 0` count
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 lands the survivor keeping `labels_applied` as a list to satisfy its retargeted exact-list tests → 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires or never fires (`[]` ≠ `0`; non-empty list truthy) → the silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op specifically even while links/comments are covered → reinstates a labels-shaped instance of the bug-3f04 class. (b) 33d0 (or 2359) normalizes `labels_applied` to an int → any exact-list label assertion 33d0 retargeted breaks → parent "no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) regresses. (c) 33d0 leaves the representation unstated → the surviving-applier payload contract (33d0) and the canary (2359) disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads.
  - impact: 33d0's parity gate and retargeted tests cannot be checked off consistently with 2359's count-canary unless 33d0 fixes the surviving payload's label representation; a silent risk to parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" (holds for links/comments but possibly not labels) and to parent "suite green" (33d0 AC4) if the list key is flipped.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload label representation between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary)
  - suggested_fix: in 33d0, fix the surviving consolidated applier's per-sub-op result representation explicitly — keep the existing list-valued `labels_applied` AND expose a derived count (e.g. `labels_applied_count` / `len(labels_applied)`) so the exact-list tests survive while an integer count exists for the invariant — and state that one representation in 33d0's parity/AC so 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate is well-typed over labels by construction across the single production path. Also structure the surviving dispatch loop so 2359 can thread per-sub-op counts in without re-architecting it.

SECONDARY (implicit shared state — recorded, not separately filed; the depends_on largely tames it): 33d0 and 2359 co-edit the SAME comment/link/label dispatch region of batch_dispatch.py update_one (L501-559) and the applier.py RECON batch_outcome line (L816-821). `2359 depends_on 33d0` sequences the work so 33d0 lands first; the residual is only that 33d0 carries no reciprocal note that 2359 will re-touch this region to add counters/canary, and 33d0 should leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so per-sub-op counts thread in cleanly — folded into the suggested_fix above.

Modes that read BENIGN (not filed): ordering violation — RESOLVED via `2359 depends_on 33d0` (collapse to one path first, then instrument the single survivor). Dependency gap (on inputs) — 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so unlike 822a/59ef (which correctly declare depends_on fe57) its lack of an input dep is appropriate. Scope overlap — 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect) vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip) vs 2359 AC2 (computed>0&&applied==0 canary) are distinct intents over the same production path; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→story E/2359) consistently. Consumer impact — once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; benign given 33d0 lands first per the dependency graph. Residual references — 33d0 explicitly deletes the dead typed leaf AND its now-misleading tests (33d0 AC1 + the parity-before-delete gate), so it does not strand references; siblings 8937/fe57/59ef key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface, not the deleted typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail matter is a G3 coverage question (and is owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.
